title: Cydista aequinoctialis
taxonomy: Cydista aequinoctialis is a species within the family Apocynaceae. Cydista aequinoctialis is native to tropical Asia, specifically India and Southeast Asia. This plant is classified as a shrub or small tree. Further classification includes it being part of the order Gentianales. Cydista aequinoctialis is also known as the 'African Trumpet Vine' or 'Bitter Apple' due to its toxic seeds.
uses: Cydista aequinoctialis, also known as the African eggplant or egg plant, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and fruits are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. In some parts of Africa, the plant is also used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Additionally, the plant's leaves have been used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, due to their astringent and antispasmodic properties. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Cydista aequinoctialis in treating various health conditions.
